note:
作为一个30岁以上的人，我也对20世纪80年代的电影情有独钟：比如印第安纳·琼斯(Indiana Jones)和费里斯·布勒(Ferris Bueller)，无数的高中电影。但40年后，对于其中一些电影来说，每当你开始重看时，玫瑰色的眼镜可能很快就会碎掉。印地到处跑，抢走可能应该掌握在当地领导人手中的文物，不是吗？费里斯·布勒把他的女朋友和最好的朋友当垃圾对待。而且，天哪，像“书呆子的复仇”这样的电影如果在本世纪上映，100年后是不会被批准的。
所有这一切，再加上印第安纳·琼斯和水晶骷髅王国--当传闻已久的比尔和泰德终于在2019年3月转变为现实时，人们做出了正确的反应。
有点像一部伙伴喜剧、高中懒汉电影和时空旅行冒险，所有这些都被包装在一个荒谬的包装中，最初的优秀冒险仍然是我崇拜的东西。(吃麦片去电话亭；有成吉思·卡恩的动作人偶。)。最近再看一遍，这部电影令人耳目一新，比普通的80年代电影表现更好。除了中世纪的一些不恰当的词语(坦率地说，这似乎不符合西奥多·洛根和比尔·普雷斯顿·埃斯克的性格。即使考虑到这部电影的上映年份，比尔和泰德仍然反讽地彼此相爱，他们尊重来自其他文化的人(至少他们知道如何尊重)，而且他们从不假装强硬或酷。即使是一些微妙的信息也能站得住脚：权威人士-从警察父亲到热爱圣代的拿破仑-可能是混蛋。音乐或艺术可以起到很好的凝聚力。即使是那些命中注定要拯救地球的人，也可以时不时地做一些小小的练习。
因此，比尔和泰德直面后果--这部系列最新电影于本周末同时在影院和视频点播上映(大多数平台的租赁费约为20美元)--无缝地将我们最优秀的英雄们转移到现代，或许并不令人惊讶。如果你没有意识到怪事可能会发生在K圈，你会想先看看原版(既是为了欣赏一些参考资料/笑话，也是为了对正在发生的事情有一个基本的了解)。但如果你也已经对怀尔德·斯塔林(Wyld Stallyns)夫妇情有独钟，那么就跳上最近的电话亭，拨通无限的电话，准备另一次迷人的、夸张的、令人惊讶的辛酸时光之旅。
直面后果发生在虚假之旅事件发生25年后，比尔(亚历克斯·温特饰)和泰德(基努·里夫斯饰)坚持他们塑造历史的音乐事业。只是，它并没有像预言所暗示的那样完全消失。你有什么要为自己辩护的吗？&#34；伟大的领袖(霍兰德·泰勒饰)在试图提醒我们的英雄们他们在这里的目标(写一首团结人类和所有人的歌曲)时说。一个月前，你在加州为40个人演奏了一场麋鹿小屋，他们大多在那里度过了2美元的玉米饼之夜，无论这意味着什么。当我们第一次看到这对二人组在一个非常家庭的婚礼上表演时，他们似乎进入了一个实验性的噪音摇滚阶段。特雷曼。喉咙在唱歌。这些作品。
不幸的是，比尔和泰德似乎只是漫无目的地享受对方的陪伴和节奏吉他工作，浪费了他们潜在的素数。他们与公主的婚姻需要夫妻治疗。洛根局长(小哈尔·兰登)。返回！)。认为这些男孩现在是更多的游手好闲的人，因为他们有时间旅行的传奇故事，伴随着不存在的职业和雄心。生活已经变得足够压抑，以至于泰德最终考虑卖掉他标志性的莱斯·保罗(Les Paul)。
但并不是每个人都失去了信心。在当地，这些家伙已经成了父亲，他们的女儿碧莉·洛根(Brigette Lundy-Paine，Netflix的非典型性人物)和西娅·斯奎尔(Thea Esquire，山姆·韦弗，SMILF)仍然认为这两位年迈的摇滚歌手是最优秀的。女孩们也和她们的父亲一样热爱音乐，但在百科全书般的知识方面，她们已经超过了她们的父母。Billie和Thea可以在即使是最粗糙的Stallyns曲目中找到低音介绍或独奏部分大小的亮点。
未来也不会完全抛弃这些音速救星。这位伟大的领袖认为比尔和泰德写这首歌的时间已经不多了，所以也许只派一个机器人去谋杀他们可以挽救已经变得多么扭曲的历史和现实。但鲁弗斯的女儿凯莉(克里斯汀·夏尔，鲍勃·汉堡店)认为，这两个人需要的是最后一次跳进电话亭重整旗鼓的机会。当她为圣迪马斯租了一个跨次元的吊舱，让比尔和泰德知道时间已经不多了，比赛终于开始了，要么送出一位救世主单身--要么努力死去。
说更多关于这个故事的话可能会破坏乐趣，并直面后果的存在就是为了带来乐趣。“直面后果”比“卓越之旅”少了一些延长的历史时间和地点冒险，比“虚假之旅”少了一点地狱妖怪，但这两个想法都被纳入了新的一期。如果原著中你最喜欢的部分涉及比尔和泰德通过时间旅行与其他主题互动，另一方面，这一部分变成了11(混合音乐电影的隐喻)。里夫斯和温特反复证明，经过这么多年和许多不同的好莱坞演出，他们并没有失去他们的喜剧天赋，其中一些镜头是这部电影最滑稽(最愚蠢)的时刻之一。
在比尔和泰德的世界里，所有新增加的演员都感觉很自然，但特别是伦迪-潘恩和韦弗真的很公正地对待比利和西娅。他们的角色是更年轻、更有能力的女性克隆他们的父亲，这些演员钉住了年轻的温特和里夫斯的语气和举止。我的脑海里发誓，1989年我听到基努·里维斯(Keanu Reeves)反复说“爸爸们！”(Kid Cudi作为Kid Cudi，但在最受欢迎的新产品中，量子纠缠领域排名紧随其后。)。
最迷人的是，现在的三部曲中的这一条目延续了那些老电影的精神。不是比尔和泰德毫不讽刺地相爱(仍然是一件事，即使这会危及他们的婚姻)，而是直面后果，将这种毫不掩饰的关怀延伸到父女关系上。“相亲相爱”的口头禅显然已经传了一代人，这是基于比莉和西娅与他们遇到的各种过去和现在的人物的互动方式。最后，无论是比尔、泰德、比利、西娅还是送命机器人，任何人都不会假装自己不是。在这个极好的扩展的世界里，你总是可以随心所欲地来(即使你更喜欢80年代的魅力摇滚，而不是90年代初的垃圾音乐)。
直面后果，甚至设法偷偷地加入了一些极端的2020年的情绪(尽管剧本早在我们新冠肺炎出现之前就出现了)。比尔和特德谈到了现代劳动力的稳步发展，他们的工作时间增加了，但实际工资却没有增加(比尔认为，挽救他们的婚姻需要他们坚守给一切带来压力的乐队：这就是为什么我们必须继续工作，他说。这不就是问题所在吗？泰德回答。是的，但这也是解决方案。)。凯利反映了代沟焦虑，因为世界正在努力应对几十年前播下的问题，从气候变化到系统性种族主义(我正在努力拯救世界母亲，但你们这一代人并没有做到这一点)。
但归根结底，面对这些提议的后果，最令人鼓舞的信息是我们两位英雄的1万英尺视角。“直面后果”最初是一个关于未兑现承诺的故事：两个家伙相信他们可以做大事，并开始努力让它成为现实，但只有生活发生了。突然，几年后，宏伟的目标没有实现，事情似乎停滞不前。如果画面中有不那么令人钦佩的角色，那么这就是可能导致一部过于黑暗的DC超级英雄电影的那种起点。但是和比尔和泰德在一起，即使是地狱之旅也增强了希望。改变世界永远不会太晚(或者改变你全人类影响生活的轨迹)，特别是如果你相信赋予下一代权力，相信20世纪80年代歌剧摇滚的永恒吸引力。伙计们，派对开始了(在现实世界中，让我们希望现场音乐最终能安全地回归，继续团结人类的小团体，怀尔德·斯塔林斯(Wyld Stallyns)风格)。
note_en:
As a person over the age of 30, I, too, have a soft spot for 1980s cinema:  Indiana Jones,  Ferris Bueller, an infinite number of high school movies. But 40 years later for some of these films, rose-colored glasses can quickly shatter whenever you start a rewatch. Indy runs around snatching up artifacts that should probably be in the hands of local leaders,  no? Ferris Bueller treats his girlfriend and best friend like  trash. And, good lord, things like  Revenge of the Nerds wouldn't be greenlit in 100 years if pitched this century.
 All of that—plus  Indiana Jones and the Kingdom of the Crystal Skull—made "caution" the correct reaction when the long-rumored " Bill & Ted 3" finally transitioned to reality in  March 2019.
 Kind of a buddy comedy, high school slacker movie, and time travel adventure all wrapped into one absurd package, the original  Excellent Adventure remains something I adore. (Ate the  cereal to get the phone booth; had the  Genghis Kahn action figure.) And rewatching it recently, that film refreshingly holds up better than your average '80s flick. Outside of some ill-chosen words in medieval times (which frankly  seems out of character for Theodore Logan and Bill Preston Esq. even considering the film's release year), Bill and Ted unironically love each other, they treat folks from other cultures with respect (as best they know how to, at least), and they never pretend to be anything close to tough or cool. Even some of the subtle messaging holds up: People in authority—from a cop dad to a sundae-loving Napoleon—can be jerks. Music or the arts can be a great unifier. Even those with pre-destined, Earth-saving greatness can  use a little practice from time to time.
 So maybe it shouldn't be a surprise that  Bill and Ted Face the Music—the series' latest film simultaneously released this weekend in theaters and via VOD ( ~$20 to rent on most platforms)—so seamlessly shifts our most excellent heroes into modern times. If you're not aware that  strange things can be afoot at the Circle K, you'll want to  watch the originals first (both to enjoy some references/in-jokes  and to have a basic grasp of what's happening). But if you also already have a soft spot for the Wyld Stallyns, hop in your nearest phone booth, dial up the infinite, and prepare for another charming, over-the-top and surprisingly poignant ride through time.
  Face the Music takes place 25 years after the events of  Bogus Journey, and Bill (Alex Winter) and Ted (Keanu Reeves) have stuck with their history-shaping music careers. Only, it hasn't quite gone as the prophecy hinted. "What have you got to say for yourself?" The Great Leader (Holland Taylor) says at one point while trying to remind our heroes of their goals here (writing a song that will unite humanity and all). "One month ago, you played an Elks Lodge in California to 40 people, who were mostly there for $2 taco night, whatever that means." When we first see the duo—performing at a  very familial wedding—they appear to be into an experimental noise rock phase. Theremin. Throat singing. The works.
 Unfortunately, Bill and Ted seem to have kind of wasted their potential primes just aimlessly enjoying each other's company and rhythm guitar work. Their marriages to the princesses need couples therapy. Chief Logan (Hal Landon Jr. returns!) thinks of these boy-men as even more deadbeats now, as they have tall tales of time traveling to accompany non-existent careers and ambitions. Life has gotten depressing enough that Ted considers finally selling his signature Les Paul.
 But not everyone has lost the faith. Locally, the dudes have become dads, and their daughters Billie Logan ( Brigette Lundy-Paine, Netflix's  Atypical) and Thea Esquire ( Sam Weaver,  SMILF) still see the two aging rockers as most excellent. The girls also share their fathers' passion for music, but they've already surpassed their parents when it comes to encyclopedic knowledge of it. Billie and Thea can find the bass intro- or solo section-sized bright spots in even the roughest Stallyns tracks.
 The future hasn't quite abandoned these sonic saviors, either. The Great Leader thinks time has all but run out for Bill and Ted to write  the song, so maybe only sending a robot to murder them will save how warped history and reality have become. But Rufus' daughter Kelly (Kristen Schaal,  Bob's Burgers) thinks all the duo needs is one last chance to hop in the phone booth to right the ship. When she charters an interdimensional pod for San Dimas to let Bill and Ted know time has started to run out, the race is on to finally deliver a savior single—or die trying.
   Saying anything more about the story might spoil the fun, and  Face the Music exists to deliver fun.  Face the Music has a bit less of the extended historic time and place adventures than  Excellent Journey and a bit less of the hell hyjinx than  Bogus Journey, but both of those ideas factor into this new installment. If your favorite part of the originals involved Bill and Ted interacting with other themes via time travel, on the other hand, that bit gets turned up to 11 (to mix music movie metaphors). Reeves and Winter demonstrate repeatedly they haven't lost their comedy chops after all the years and many divergent Hollywood gigs, and some of these sequences stand among the film's funniest (silliest) moments.
 All of the new cast additions feel natural in the  Bill & Ted universe, but in particular Lundy-Paine and Weaver really do Billie and Thea justice. Their characters are younger, more capable female clones of their dads, and these actors nail the intonation and demeanor of young Winter and Reeves. My mind swears it heard 1989 Keanu Reeves saying "dads!" repeatedly. (Kid Cudi as "Kid Cudi, but into quantum entanglement" ranks a close second in terms of favorite new additions.)
 Most charming of all, this entry into the now-trilogy continues the ethos of those old films. Instead of Bill and Ted loving each other unironically (still a thing, even if it jeopardizes their marriage),  Face the Music extends that unabashed care to father-daughter relationships, too. The "be excellent to each other" mantra has clearly been passed down a generation based on how Billie and Thea interact with the various past and present figures they encounter. And in the end, no one from Bill and Ted and Billie and Thea to Death or a Death-delivering robot ever pretends to be anything they're not. In this excellent expanded universe, you can always come as you are (even if you prefer 1980s glam rock to early '90s grunge).
 Face the Music even manages to sneak in a few extremely 2020 sentiments (though the script came about long before our COVID-19 existence did). Bill and Ted speak to the steady march of modern labor where hours increase but real wages don't (Bill believes saving their marriages requires them to stick with the band that's put a strain on everything: "That's why we have to keep working," he says. "Isn't that the problem?" Ted replies. "Yes, but it's also the solution"). Kelly reflects generational divide angst as the world grapples with problems sowed decades ago, from climate change to systemic racism ("I'm trying to save the world mother, it's not like your generation has done that great").
 But ultimately, the most encouraging message  Face the Music offers is the 10,000-foot view of our two heroes.  Face the Music is initially a story of unfulfilled promise: two dudes believed they could do big things and set about trying to make it happen, but only life happened. Suddenly it's years later, no grandiose goals have been achieved, and things seem to be in stasis. If less admirable characters were in the frame, that's the kind of starting point that could lead to an overly  dark DC superhero flick. But with Bill and Ted, even journeys through hell reinforce hope. It's never too late to change the world (or the trajectory of your all-of-humanity-impacting life), especially if you believe in empowering the next generation and the everlasting appeal of 1980s operatic rock. Party on, dudes (and in the real world, let's hope live music can eventually safely return to continue unifying small groups of humanity, Wyld Stallyns style).
title: 新比尔和泰德不会团结人类，但这是一个最棒的视频点播之夜
title_en: New Bill & Ted won’t unite humanity, but it’s a most excellent VOD night

在周四的一次全公司会议上，Facebook首席执行官马克·扎克伯格(Mark Zuckerberg)表示，一个倡导追随者携带武器参加威斯康星州基诺沙市即将举行的抗议活动的民兵页面仍然留在该平台上，原因是“一个操作失误”。该页面和相关活动引发了对该公司的广泛批评，据称一名17岁的嫌疑人周二晚间开枪打死了两名抗议者。
然而，根据BuzzFeed新闻看到的一份内部报告，与基诺沙警卫队页面相关的活动在创建后至少被Facebook标记了455次，并已被四名主持人清除，他们都认为它“没有违规”。周三，在枪击事件发生几个小时后，该页面和活动最终从平台上删除。
Facebook的一名员工在内部“暴力和煽动工作组”中写道：“要正确看待这个数字，它占了当天所有事件报道的66%。”以说明该公司收到的关于这一事件的投诉数量。
BuzzFeed新闻无法核实民兵页面或其相关活动上的内容，因为它们已从平台上删除。The Verge之前的一篇报道指出，在29岁的黑人男子雅各布·布莱克(Jacob Blake)被警察枪杀后，该页面发布了一条“战斗号召”，并接待了一些在基诺沙倡导暴力的评论者。
BuzzFeed新闻看到的这份内部报告揭示了忧心忡忡的Facebook用户在多大程度上警告该公司一个呼吁公共暴力的团体，以及该公司如何未能采取行动。该报告写道：“回想起来，这一事件非常不寻常。”报告指出，与基诺沙卫队事件的455次相比，当天第二高的事件被用户标记了18次。
周二晚上民兵聚集在基诺沙后，一名17岁的年轻人据称手持步枪杀死了两名抗议者。Facebook坚称，这名嫌疑人与基诺沙警卫队的页面或活动没有直接联系。事件发生后，嫌疑人的Facebook和Instagram个人资料被删除。
你在Facebook或其他科技公司工作吗？我们很高兴收到你的来信。联系ryan.mac@buzzfeed.com或通过我们的举报热线渠道之一。
在Facebook周四的全体会议上，扎克伯格表示，来自威斯康星州的图片“令人痛苦，真的令人沮丧”，然后他承认，该公司犯了一个错误，没有更早地撤下基诺沙警卫队的页面和活动。该页面违反了Facebook上周出台的新规定，该规定将民兵和QAnon组织列为庆祝暴力活动的“危险个人和组织”。
扎克伯格说，尽管有用户报告，但该公司并没有抓住这个页面，因为这些投诉已经发送给了内容审核承包商，他们不精通“某些民兵”是如何运作的。“经过第二次审查后，负责危险组织的团队意识到这违反了政策，我们将其删除。”
在谈话中，Facebook员工猛烈抨击扎克伯格继续允许仇恨在该平台上传播。
一位Facebook用户在试图举报基诺沙警卫队的Facebook页面，指出它没有违反我们的某一特定社区标准后，收到了这一回应。
“在什么情况下，我们应该承担责任，让充满仇恨的愤怒在我们的服务中蔓延？”一名员工写道。[A]NTI的犹太主义、阴谋论和白人至上主义在我们的服务中弥漫着臭味。
BuzzFeed新闻看到的内部报告更多地揭示了Facebook的失败。
“组织者…。在活动描述中倡导与会者携带武器参加活动，“内部报告写道。“有多篇新闻报道称，我们迟迟没有撤下这项活动。”
根据一名Facebook用户发送给BuzzFeed新闻的一张屏幕截图，他在基诺沙警卫队的页面上标记出“一个可信的暴力威胁”，被告知“这不违反我们特定的社区标准之一”。
除了确定基诺沙警卫队页面是否是非暴力的四项人工审查外，Facebook的报告还指出，多项“由自动化处理”的审查也得出了同样的结论。作为拟议改革的一部分，撰写这份报告的Facebook员工表示，公司应该监控事件反馈报告的激增情况，并“鉴于这已被证明是迫在眉睫的伤害的良好信号，立即启动调查”。
这位工作人员写道：“这篇帖子提供了更多关于发生了什么的细节，以及我们正在做出的改变，以更快地发现和调查类似的事件。”“这是一个发人深省的提醒，提醒人们我们所做工作的重要性，特别是在这段充满压力的时期。”
note_en:
In a companywide meeting on Thursday, Facebook CEO Mark Zuckerberg said that a militia page advocating for followers to bring weapons to an upcoming protest in Kenosha, Wisconsin, remained on the platform because of “an operational mistake.” The page and an associated event inspired widespread criticism of the company after  a 17-year-old suspect allegedly shot and killed two protesters Tuesday night.
 The event associated with the Kenosha Guard page, however, was flagged to Facebook at least 455 times after its creation, according to an internal report viewed by BuzzFeed News, and had been cleared by four moderators, all of whom deemed it “non-violating.” The page and event were eventually removed from the platform on Wednesday — several hours after the shooting.
    “To put that number into perspective, it made up 66% of all event reports that day,” one Facebook worker wrote in the internal “Violence and Incitement Working Group” to illustrate the number of complaints the company had received about the event.
 BuzzFeed News could not verify the content on the militia page or its associated event because they had been removed from the platform. A previous story from  the Verge noted that the page had issued a “call to arms” and hosted a number of commenters advocating for violence in Kenosha following  the police shooting of 29-year-old Black man Jacob Blake.
  The internal report seen by BuzzFeed News reveals the extent to which concerned Facebook users went to warn the company of a group calling for public violence, and how the company failed to act. “The event is highly unusual in retrospect,” reads the report, which notes that the next highest event for the day had been flagged 18 times by users compared to the 455 times of the Kenosha Guard event.
 After militia gathered in Kenosha on Tuesday night, a 17-year-old with a rifle allegedly killed two protesters. Facebook has maintained that the suspect, whose Facebook and Instagram profiles were taken down after the incident, had no direct connection with the Kenosha Guard page or event.
 Do you work at Facebook or another technology company? We'd love to hear from you. Reach out at   ryan.mac@buzzfeed.com  or  via one of our   tip line channels .
 During Facebook’s Thursday all-hands meeting, Zuckerberg said that the images from Wisconsin were “painful and really discouraging,” before acknowledging that the company had made a mistake in not taking the Kenosha Guard page and event down sooner. The page had violated Facebook’s new rules  introduced last week that labeled militia and QAnon groups as “Dangerous Individuals and Organizations” for their celebrations of violence.
 The company did not catch the page despite user reports, Zuckerberg said, because the complaints had been sent to content moderation contractors who were not versed in “how certain militias” operate. “On second review, doing it more sensitively, the team that was responsible for dangerous organizations recognized that this violated the policies and we took it down.”
 During the talk, Facebook employees hammered Zuckerberg for continuing to allow the spread of hatred on the platform.
  One Facebook user received this response after trying to report the Kenosha Guard Facebook page noting that it did not "go against one of our specific Community Standards."
  “At what point do we take responsibility for enabling hate filled bile to spread across our services?” wrote one employee. “[A]nti semitism, conspiracy, and white supremacy reeks across our services.”
 The internal report seen by BuzzFeed News sheds more light on Facebook’s failure.
 “Organizers… advocated for attendees to bring weapons to an event in the event description,” the internal report reads. “There are multiple news articles about our delay in taking down the event.”
 One Facebook user who flagged the Kenosha Guard page “for a credible threat of violence” was told “it doesn’t go against one of our specific Community Standards,” according to a screenshot they sent to BuzzFeed News.
 In addition to the four manual reviews that determined the Kenosha Guard page to be non-violative, the Facebook report also noted a number of reviews that “were handled by automation” had reached the same conclusion. As part of a proposed change, the Facebook employee writing the report said that the company should monitor spikes in feedback reports for events and “trigger investigation immediately given this has proved to be a good signal for imminent harm.”
  “This post provides more details around what happened and changes we are making to detect and investigate similar events sooner,” the worker wrote. “This is a sobering reminder of the importance of the work we do, especially during this charged period.”
title: Facebook的基诺沙卫队民兵活动被报道了455次。主持人说很好。
title_en: Facebook’s Kenosha Guard Militia Event Was Reported 455 Times. Moderators Said It Was Fine.

我学过的最重要的色彩技巧之一就是避免在我的作品中使用黑色。我儿时的美术老师扎穆拉夫人在我上中学的时候第一次警告我要提防黑人。在RISD我又多次听到同样的话。乍听起来很奇怪，但这是个好建议。
问题是，我们看到黑暗的东西，并认为它们是黑色的东西。事实上，很难找到纯黑色的东西。道路不是黑色的。你的办公椅不是黑色的。Sparrow中的侧栏不是黑色的。网页上的文字不是黑色的。
高中时，我和其他几个热爱艺术的朋友一起在美术室里度过了很多空闲时间。(要么是那样，要么是玩光环，我也做了很多次。)。扎穆拉夫人不断地向我们展示不同艺术家的作品。她经常带回来翻阅的一本书是韦恩·蒂波特的作品。她会兴奋地指给他看他在阴影间滑过的亮色原色。她会让我们看看他明亮的蓝色影子！
我可能把那本书看了十遍。Thiebaud是阴影不是黑色的完美例子。(他是一位画家，任何住在旧金山的人都应该知道。)。
他的影子是他绘画中最饱和的部分，这也出现在屏幕上。在纸面上，它们看起来会更漂亮。在现实生活中更美好。
现在你可能会想，“是的，但那些是画。它们不是真的。“。你觉得我们和提波特的接口有多真实？
但我一定也在想同样的事情，因为有一天在美术课上，扎穆拉太太拿着一个蓝色灯泡进来证明了这一点。她把灯泡拧成夹灯，插上电源，把它夹在凳子上。然后，她从装满纯白东西的柜子里拿出一个纯白的球(她用来做人物画练习)，把它放在一个纯白的基座上，在灯光下。果然，当她打开蓝灯时，球投下的阴影是橙色的，而不是黑色的。
我大吃一惊。现在，当我走在旧金山的街道上时，我喜欢寻找我能找到的最饱和的影子。这是我用PATH在街上拍的一张照片(是的，Filters…)。这首先让我想起了蒂波特：
那张照片中最黑暗的部分是什么？不是000000号。是130f30号。(即19%的亮度和69%的饱和度！)。
当你把纯黑色放在一组精心挑选的颜色旁边时，黑色会压倒一切。它之所以脱颖而出是因为它不是天然的。你周围所有的“黑色”日常物品都有一定数量的光从它们身上反射，这意味着它们不是黑色的，而是深灰色的。而且那道光可能有一种颜色，所以它们甚至不是深灰色的，而是彩色的--深灰色。
我们日常使用的很多应用程序都有黑色，它们不是真正的黑色，而是深灰色。Twitter的侧边栏，Sublime Text 2的侧边栏，如果你安装了苏打水(你应该安装的话！)，新的Photoshop的背景，日历小工具。甚至连Twitter Bootstrap也是如此。它们都使用接近黑色的颜色，但略微柔和，这样就不会压倒屏幕上的其他元素。
下面是一个人为设计的示例…。Dribble有一个非常棒的功能，可以让你按颜色搜索镜头。如果你搜索纯黑色和“真正黑色”的照片，哪一种感觉更好？不是纯黑色的。
这不仅仅是关于颜色的亮度(或值)，也不是…。
你也可以做得比远离纯黑#000000更好。每当你处理灰色时，给它们加一点颜色，它们就不会那么暗淡了。您可以添加的颜色量与颜色的深浅程度成正比。我的路径照片中的黑色有69%！饱和度，而浅灰色可能只需要3%。
在为Segment制作调色板时，我将其用作一般指南。我在我们的灰色中混合了少量的橙黄色。对于最浅的灰色，饱和度从2%开始，然后稳步增加，直到最深的灰色达到22%，在Photoshop拾色器上形成一个弧形。
但不要再相信我的话了-让我们看看Facebook。为什么Facebook的移动界面感觉这么好？因为所有的灰色都充满了脸书蓝。
扎穆拉夫人在中学时第一次警告我要提防黑人，后来也有人警告我，但我还是花了很长时间才真正摸索，并建立起对它的自动反应。即使是现在，我仍然很难选择能营造正确情绪的灰色。
底线是：当你在颜色选择器中发现#000000的时候，问问你自己，你是否真的想要纯黑色。吃点更自然的东西可能会让你过得更好。如果你喜欢冒险，试着完全远离拾色器的左边。
note_en:
One of the most important color tricks I’ve ever learned was to avoid using the color black in my work. Mrs. Zamula, my childhood art teacher, first warned me about black when I was in middle school. And I heard the same again multiple times at RISD. It sounds weird at first, but it’s good advice.
 Problem is, we see  dark things and assume they are  black things. When, in reality, it’s very hard to find something that is pure black. Roads aren’t black. Your office chair isn’t black. The sidebar in Sparrow isn’t black. Words on web pages aren’t black.
  In high school, I spent lots of my free time in the art room with a few other art-loving friends. (It was either that or playing Halo, which I did a lot of too.) Mrs. Zamula was constantly showing us different artists’ work. One book she would always bring back out to leaf through was of  Wayne Thiebaud’s work. She would excitedly point out the slivers of bright primary color he slips between shadows. And she’d tell us to look at his bright blue shadows!
 I probably went through that book ten times. Thiebaud is a perfect example of shadows not being black. (And he’s a painter that anyone living in San Francisco should know.)
  His shadows are some of the most saturated parts of his paintings, and that’s on the screen too. On paper they’d look even nicer. And in real-life nicer still.
 Now you might be thinking, “Yeah, but those are paintings. They’re not real.” Well, how real do you think our interfaces are to Thiebaud?
 But I must have been thinking the same thing, because one of those days in art class Mrs. Zamula came in with a blue light bulb to prove it. She screwed the bulb into a clamp-light, plugged the light in and clamped it to a stool. Then she got a pure-white ball from her cabinet full of pure-white things (that she kept for figure drawing exercises) and placed it on a pure-white pedestal, under the light. And sure enough, when she turned on the blue light, the shadow cast by the ball was an orange tint, not black.
 I was amazed. And now when I walk down the street in San Francisco I love looking for the most saturated shadow I can find. Here’s a photo I took down the street with Path (yes, filters…) that reminded me of Thiebaud in the first place:
   The darkest part of that image? It’s not  #000000. It’s  #130f30. (That’s 19% brightness and 69% saturation!)
  When you put pure black next to a set of meticulously picked colors, the black overpowers everything else. It stands out because it’s not natural. All of the “black” everyday objects around you have some amount of light bouncing off of them, which means they aren’t black, they’re dark gray. And that light probably has a tint to it, so they’re not even dark gray, they’re colored-dark gray.
 Lots of the apps we use on a daily basis have blacks that aren’t really blacks, but dark grays. Twitter’s sidebar,  Sublime Text 2's sidebar if you have  Soda Dark installed (which you should!), new Photoshop’s background, the calendar widget. Even Twitter Bootstrap. They all use colors close to black, but slightly muted so they don’t overpower the rest of the elements on the screen.
  Here’s a bit of a contrived example… Dribbble has a pretty awesome feature that lets you search for shots by color. If you search for  shots with pure black and  shots with “real black” which ones feel better? Not the ones with pure black in them.
 It’s not only about the brightness (or value) of the color either…
  You can do even better than staying away from pure  #000000 black too. Whenever you’re working with grays, add a bit of color to them and they will feel less dull. The amount of color you can add is proportional to how dark the color is. The black from my Path photo had 69%! saturation, whereas a light-gray might only need 3%.
 I’ve used that as a general guide when making the color palette for  Segment. I mix a small amount of yellow-orange into our grays. Saturation starts at 2% for our lightest gray and steadily increases until it’s at 22% for the darkest gray, forming an arc across the Photoshop color picker.
 But don’t take my word for it again—let’s look at Facebook. Why does the Facebook Mobile interface feel so nice? Because all of those grays are pumped full of Facebook Blue.
   Mrs. Zamula first warned me about black back in middle school, and others warned me later on, but it’s still taken me a long time to really grok, and to build up the automatic response against it. Even now, I still have trouble choosing grays that set the right mood.
 Bottom line is: when you find  #000000 in your color picker, ask yourself if you really want pure black. You’re probably better off with something more natural. And if you’re feeling adventurous, try staying away from the left edge of the color picker altogether.
title: 设计提示：切勿使用黑色(2012)

note:
当谈到涉及不明飞行物的案件时，很少有案件像1975年洛林事件那样被记录得如此充分，涉及的人如此之多。
尽管美国各地敏感地区不明原因侵入领空的话题受到了新的关注，但这些类型的奇怪事件并不一定是新的。对这一事件或一系列事件最令人费解的描述之一，发生在冷战最严重的时期。
1975年的一系列夜晚，缅因州的洛林空军基地遭到源自加拿大领空的神秘飞船的入侵。当时，该基地是B-52轰炸机和KC-135加油机的所在地，承担着核警报任务。洛林空军基地的事件在个人证词和解密的中央情报局和国家军事指挥中心(NMCC)文件方面都有非常好的记录。让这些事件变得如此有趣的还有，有多少人参与或知道了潜在的威胁和对此的反应。这确实是一场社区规模的考验，甚至登上了全国媒体。考虑到我们正在谈论的是一个存放核武器的基地，以及这些武器的运载系统，他们所依赖的轰炸机和加油机，至少可以说，对这些奇怪的入侵的担忧是极端的。
1975年秋天在洛林空军基地发生的离奇事件还有一个有趣的地方，那就是其他地方正在发生的事情。根据更多的官方文件和报告，在这段时间内，类似的事件非常普遍，尽管很少有(如果有的话)具有如此广泛的经验或如此公开的性质。
那是1975年的10月。当时，洛林空军基地是一个战略空军司令部(SAC)基地，拥有两个KC-135加油机中队和一个B-52轰炸机中队，负责警戒核武器交付任务。这是SAC在美国大陆最东边的基地，使其在快速应对危机方面处于独特的地位。
这件奇怪的事情始于10月27日晚上，当时基地的安全人员观察到官方描述为“一架身份不明的直升机”出现在该设施的北部边界附近。据称，这架飞机在低空飞行，估计高度约为150英尺，似乎有一个红色的导航灯和一个白色的闪光灯。直升机似乎对洛林高度戒备森严的核武器储存区特别感兴趣。军方出动了国民警卫队直升机，试图联系和识别这架飞机，但事实证明这些尝试都没有成功。基地立即进入高度戒备状态。
飞机出现后不久，洛林控制塔的雷达操作员观察到另一架未知的飞机在基地东北10至13英里处盘旋。尽管多次试图通过民用和军用频道的无线电联系，但仍然无法识别这架飞机的身份。第一架未知的飞机最终转向北方，在新不伦瑞克的大瀑布附近飞入加拿大，第二架未知的飞机从雷达上消失，可能降落或下降到雷达覆盖范围以下。
第二天晚上，也就是10月28日，另一架不明飞机出现在洛林上空，这一次没有灯光。国民警卫队直升机再次被派往调查，但无法建立飞机的视觉确认。在事件发生后发布的一份行动报告(OPREP)中，官员们写道，“我们认为，这架不明身份的直升机在武器储存区表现出了明确的意图，是聪明的，是一名最有能力的飞行员。”这些事件至少持续到10月30日。
作为对入侵的回应，洛林增加了当地的安全存在，并与加拿大当局协调，允许美国飞机在不明飞机返回时追赶违规飞机进入加拿大领空。
“纽约时报”(New York Times)报道了1979年发表的“UFO档案：不为人知的故事”一文中的入侵事件，并补充说，尽管参谋长联席会议收到了多次关于入侵的简报，但空军随后对缅因州洛林空军基地目击事件的调查没有揭示目击事件的原因。缅因州洛林空军基地就是这一系列引人注目的事件的发源地。这篇文章还补充说，尽管参谋长联席会议收到了多次关于入侵的简报，但空军随后对发生在缅因州洛林空军基地的目击事件进行了调查，并没有揭示目击事件的原因。
对洛林空袭事件的一种描述来自阿瑟·比尔斯(Arthur Beers)，他在1970年至1976年期间在该基地服役。在LoringRembers.com上发布的一篇文章中，比尔斯描述了他在洛林的“最难忘的经历”。LoringRembers.com是一个致力于记录许多在洛林服役的男男女女的经历的网站。
也许我最喜欢的故事是当我作为保安警察值班指挥官出现在“评级副刊”的时候。一天晚上，我正在值班，这时核武器储存区被一架不明身份的直升机嗡嗡作响。很明显，我们报告了这个上行频道，然后我跑到储藏区。当我到达的时候，我被告知国家紧急指挥所有电话找我。当我与国家指挥所的上校交谈并解释我的团队在储藏区报告的情况时，他命令我，如果直升机再次试图在该地区嗡嗡作响，就将其击落。
我立即与我的SAT团队就这些命令进行了简报，但在我完成简报之前，我被叫回了入口点，在那里我与一名将军交谈，他说总统已经听取了简报，我的新命令是只有在直升机试图降落时才开枪(啊，见鬼！)。我出去重新向我的团队汇报情况，在关键时刻，一名不知名的飞行员乘坐一架未点亮的休伊型直升机又回来了，然后在我值班的时候又回来了两次。他另外三晚回来然后就消失了。据我所知，这个身份从未被发现。
有趣的是，一旦北美防空司令部得到信息，我们未经授权飞越了存储区，北美防空司令部就发射了在洛林处于戒备状态的F-106战机。不太确定那个喷气式飞机骑师将如何看到或射击一架直升机，在距离地面50英尺的缅因州北部森林中，夜间在没有灯光的情况下飞行，但我猜北美防空司令部必须做些什么。我没见过F-106，也没见过直升机。
其他个人证词使入侵者实际上是直升机的说法变得复杂。迈克尔·华莱士(Michael Wallace)曾是KC-135加油机飞行员，1975年驻扎在洛林，他在YouTube上分享了自己离奇的洛林入侵经历：
华莱士说，他听取了关于入侵洛林拥有核武器的B-52战机和武器储存设施的简报。华莱士和数百名其他人员被告知，一个无声的发光物体在基地上空盘旋，它可以非常迅速地移动，而且不同寻常的是，在垂直运动的情况下，快速的直线运动可以在没有任何明显半径的情况下转弯。这是一项令人难以置信的技术。基地人员公开称该物体为不明飞行物。
华莱士接着声称，洛林的工作人员听取了简报，只是向SAC官员讲述了这次入侵，而不是向媒体发表讲话，他们打算告诉媒体，有一架加拿大直升机越过边境骚扰我们。他还指出，将派出拦截器，以协助保护基地和调查正在发生的事情。
当基地指挥所通知华莱士切换无线电频率时，华莱士最终被派用他的KC-135执行加油任务，以支持F-4幻影进行无关的训练。飞行中的领队KC-135接到指示，离开编队，关灯，关闭无线电，自行决定前往洛林，华莱士称这是一个非常不寻常的命令。
华莱士记得，当飞行员和塔台工作人员试图追踪这个物体时，无线电里传来紧张的声音，因为它似乎以令人难以置信的速度在基地上空来回飞行。通讯一开始，它们就结束了，塔台的工作人员简单地说，我们已经失去了它。当华莱士后来看到被派去拦截该物体的领头飞机的飞行员时，他告诉华莱士，我不能谈论这件事，如果我能谈论这件事，你也不会相信我。
LoringRemembers.com包含了许多关于1975年入侵的其他参考，许多前Loring人员称这一事件是他们最难忘的经历。一名前野战维修中队成员记得，在入侵的当晚，飞行线路上的活动是一场疯狂的活动。
约翰·E·莫卡维奇(John E.Morkavich)曾于1972年至1975年在洛林工作，他向LoringRemembers.com讲述了以下内容：
我是一名医院医务兵或技术72-75，73年秋天的一个晚上(如果其他人回忆起来，有更好的时间线[原文如此]，我会接受纠正的)基地警报响起，他们说那是[原文原文]大的要紧急调遣喷气式飞机和轰炸机。我们大约6个人登上医疗营的屋顶，等待一切。基地变得疯狂，克莱克逊人，警报器，安全警车在许多闪光灯周围飞驰。飞机航线上传来发动机的轰鸣声。嗯，大约3个星期后，我和约翰·P·谢泼德博士一起在军官俱乐部，几名飞行员和我们坐在一起。我们谈论了体育和政治，然后谢泼德医生问那天晚上发生了什么事。他们用无处不在的问题回答，你有必要知道吗？&#34；谢泼德说该死的有，所以我听到了这个解释，两位飞行员都非常认真。
有一个不明飞行物不知从哪里冒出来，在武器储存设施附近的东洛林上空盘旋。飞机被紧急拦截，这是肉眼可以看到的，并在雷达上进行跟踪。然后中校说。这该死的东西前一秒还在那里，下一秒就消失了……然后雷达分析显示，这个怪物离我们太远了，而且在这么高的高度……让我告诉你--34；。这个星球上没有任何东西能做这架飞机或该死的UFO所做的事情。然后他们站起来离开了桌子。谢泼德和我都惊呆了，我们再也没有谈论过这件事。这不是胡说八道，我欢迎那些在那里并记得的人。
LoringRemembers.com甚至有一个名为UFO官方故事的栏目，上面简单地说，1975年，一架UFO在WSA的几个晚上里引起了轰动。互联网上的传说已经证明这是一个真正的UFO阴谋，涉及许多其他基地。国防部发布了一份关于这一事件的解密报告。
巴里·格林伍德(Barry Greenwood)和劳伦斯·福塞特(Lawrence Fawcett)对洛林事件进行了最彻底的调查之一，并将其收录在他们1984年出版的“清晰的意图：政府掩盖UFO经历”一书中。这本书的节选包含了关于洛林的章节，可以在这里在线找到。它非常详细地说明了事件的叙述是什么，至少根据他们的研究是这样的。我们会在第二天晚上来取，但我们鼓励您阅读整个节选。
1975年10月28日，晚上7点45分，分配到第42保安警察中队的克利夫顿·W·布拉克斯利中士和威廉·J·朗上士在军火库值班。还有丹尼·刘易斯中士都是中士。布莱克斯利和朗在3000英尺的高空发现了似乎是一架飞机从北部接近洛林空军基地的飞行灯光。这架飞机此时距离洛林不到3英里，在接下来的一个小时里，人们断断续续地观察到了这一点。
第一次发现这艘飞船时，刘易斯中士给指挥所打了电话，通知指挥所，这艘未知的飞船已经返回洛林。刘易斯报告说，他能看到一盏白色的闪光灯和一盏琥珀色或橙色的灯。第42轰炸联队司令再次回应。他冲到堆放场附近，观察到了这艘未知的飞船。他报告说，他在物体上看到了闪烁的白光和琥珀色的光。空中的速度和运动表明这架飞机是一架直升机。从晚上7点45分到晚上8点20分，储存区的人员一直在用肉眼和电子方式观察这艘船，控制塔雷达显示，这艘船位于洛林警戒线以北3英里(约合3公里)的位置。
这架未知的飞行器会出现又消失在人们的视线中，并一度出现在150英尺的跑道尽头。该物体随后熄灯，并再次出现在武器储存区上空，保持在150英尺的高度。
此时，B-52轰炸机机组组长史蒂文·艾克纳中士(Sgt.Steven Eichner)正与R·琼斯中士(Sgt.R.Jones)和其他机组人员一起在一辆运载火箭上工作。琼斯在飞行线路上发现了一个红色和橙色的物体。它似乎在飞行线的另一边，那里是武器储存区所在的地方。在艾克纳和琼斯看来，这个物体看起来像一个伸展开的足球。当机组人员每个人都敬畏地盯着它时，它在半空中盘旋。当他们看着的时候，这个物体熄灭了灯消失了，但很快它又在跑道的北端重新出现，以抖动的动作移动。它停了下来，盘旋着。艾克纳和其他机组人员跳上卡车，开始向该物体行驶。
沿着俄克拉荷马大道(与跑道接壤)前行，他们左转上了通往武器储存区的道路。当他们转弯时，他们发现了他们前面大约300英尺的物体。它似乎在大约五英尺高的空中盘旋，没有动静，也没有发出任何声音。该物体呈橙红色，大约有四辆车长。艾克纳描述了他接下来看到的情况：
这个物体看起来所有的颜色都混合在一起，就像你在看一个沙漠场景。你可以看到一波又一波的热浪从沙漠地面升起。这就是我看到的。物体前面有这些波浪，所有的颜色都混合在一起。这个物体是实心的，我们听不到任何噪音。
他们看不到物体上的任何门或窗，也看不到任何使物体保持在空中的螺旋桨或发动机。突然，基地恢复了活力。警报器开始尖叫。艾克纳可以看到警车上无数的蓝灯沿着飞行线和跑道高速驶向武器储存区。琼斯转过身对船员们说，我们最好离开这里！他们马上就离开了。安全警察没有试图阻止他们。他们感兴趣的是堆放场上方的物体，而不是在禁区内的卡车。机组人员将卡车开回原来的位置，并从那里观看。武器储存区的现场一片混乱，蓝光四处旋转，车辆的探照灯光束向四面八方照射。
机组人员决定不报告他们所看到的，因为他们已经进入了禁区，可能会因为违规而被逮捕。
那个物体熄灭了灯消失了，那天晚上再也看不见了。第四十二警队对武器储存区内外进行了安全检查，没有任何结果。雷达再次短暂跟踪到前往新不伦瑞克大瀑布的物体，最终失去了大瀑布本身的未知。
向华盛顿的国家军事指挥中心、空军参谋长、位于马里兰州里奇堡的空军前方作战师、SAC总部和第45师空军第9师发出了优先信息，通知他们一个未知物体已经渗透到基地并一直在核储存区。
作者采访了缅因州陆军国民警卫队第112医疗连(空中救护)的一级准尉伯纳德·波林，他的任务是追踪和识别10月下旬夜间发生的UH-1上的未知飞机。Poulin告诉Fawcett，尽管有许多目击者看到和听到入侵者，但他的直升机机组人员无法获得飞机的视觉确认，但他提供了更多细节，描述了他在高度警惕神秘入侵者的情况下走进SAC基地的经历：
嗯，在地面人员开始看到或听到，如果你愿意的话，不明飞行物经过后，我们就开始执行第一次搜索任务。所以，我们会发射，我相信我们在空中花了大约40分钟寻找这个东西，想法是这是我们正在搜索的旋转式飞船。我们被地面人员引导到基地的不同地点，那里的地面人员看到或听到了它。在这段时间里，我们一直被基地雷达(专为侦测飞机而设计的交通管制雷达)跟踪，而雷达并没有对报告的物体进行绘画。地面人员会打电话说物体在这个位置，但雷达不会发现它。
嗯，不管怎样，我们四处寻找，什么也没看到。再一次，他们会打电话说他们可以在某个地方听到，我们会去那里，但看不到。然后我们就会关机，等待下一个电话。这持续了几个晚上。这又是傍晚时分或清晨时分。我记得在执行任务的第二天晚上，雷达探测到一架返回的KC-135加油机，但结果是一艘从海外返回的KC-135加油机。有人问普林：根据一些文件，地面人员报告说，有时你会把你的飞机带到距离入侵者100英尺的范围内，但你看不到它？&#34；他回答说：&#34；是的，嗯，我们可以非常低地降到他们说的地方，然后打开探照灯，扫描整个区域。活动结束后，我们讨论了我们的任务。未来的权力机构非常关心正在发生的事情，以及我们是否能够看到什么。他们一直在那里维护，你知道，那些都是非常敏感的地方，他们必须知道地狱发生了什么。
当他们到达基地时，安全盖上得非常紧，以至于两名飞行员都被允许只给妻子打一次电话，说他们正在执行任务。在与查普曼的一次会面中，波林回忆起指挥官说过的话：“我们必须掩盖这样一个事实，即有人已经能够渗透到炸弹投放场及其周围，而我们不知道发生了什么。”我们必须找出发生了什么事，并防止它再次发生。
在洛林，额外的人力已经武装起来，随时可以部署。安全警察战斗人员将在中央安全控制中心驻扎。在夜幕降临期间，在武器储存区增派了一支两人机动巡逻队，而一支10人的预备役部队正在待命，准备部署。一支由两人组成的巡逻队将部署在基地以北约一英里的关键有利位置，以增加监视。SAC/SP消息通知了北部基地情况，并建议沿美加边境设置安全选项3警报。
这条信息发给了新罕布夏州的Pease AFB、纽约的Plattsburgh AFB、密歇根州的Wurtsmith AFB、密歇根州的Kinchloe AFB、密歇根州的Sawyer AFB、北达科他州的Grand Forks AFB、北达科他州的Minot AFB、蒙大拿州的Malmstrom AFB、华盛顿的费尔柴尔德AFB和爱荷华州的巴克斯代尔AFB。这条信息的主题识别行是防御直升机袭击，内容是：
在过去的两个晚上，在我们的一个北层基地，观察到一架身份不明的直升机在武器储存区上空和附近盘旋。到目前为止，试图识别这架飞机的结果是否定的。为了核武器安全的利益，该行动。
.
note_en:
When it comes to cases involving unidentified flying objects, few are as well documented or involve as many people as what occurred at Loring in 1975.
 Despite the  newfound attention the topic of  unexplained incursions into  airspace over sensitive locales across the United States is receiving, these types of bizarre incidents are not necessarily new. One of the most puzzling accounts of such an event, or series of events, occurred during the depths of the Cold War.
 Over a series of nights in 1975, Loring Air Force Base in Maine was invaded by mysterious craft originating from Canadian airspace. At the time, the base was home to B-52 bombers and KC-135 tankers and was tasked with the nuclear alert mission. The Loring AFB incidents are extremely well documented, both in terms of personal testimonials and declassified CIA and  National Military Command Center (NMCC) documents. What also made the events so interesting was just how many people were involved or knew about the potential threat and the reaction to it. It was truly a community-scale ordeal that even made its way into the national press. Considering we are talking about a base that housed nuclear weapons and a delivery system for those weapons, the bombers and tankers they rely on, the concern regarding the strange incursions was extreme, to say the least.
  What is also interesting about the bizarre events at Loring Air Force Base in the fall of 1975 was what was going on elsewhere, as well. Based on additional official documentation and reports, similar occurrences were remarkably widespread during this time period, albeit few, if any, were as widely experienced or as public in nature.
  It was October of 1975. At the time, Loring Air Force Base was a Strategic Air Command (SAC) base that housed two KC-135 tanker squadrons and a B-52 bomber squadron, which had the alert nuclear weapons delivery mission. It was SAC's easternmost base in the continental United States, putting it in a unique position to quickly respond to a crisis.
 The strange affair began on the evening on October 27 when security personnel at the base observed what was described officially as “an unidentified helicopter” that appeared near the northern perimeter of the installation. The aircraft was said to be flying at a low altitude, estimated to be around 150 feet, and appeared to feature a red navigational light and a white strobe. The helicopter seemed to be particularly interested in the highly-secure nuclear weapons storage area at Loring. Army National Guard helicopters were dispatched in an attempt to contact and identify the aircraft, but those attempts proved unsuccessful. The base was immediately put on high alert.
  Shortly after the craft appeared, radar operators in the control tower at Loring observed another unknown aircraft circling between 10 and 13 miles northeast of the base. Once again, that aircraft could not be identified despite numerous attempts to make contact by radio on both civilian and military channels. The first unknown aircraft eventually turned north and flew into Canada near Grand Falls, New Brunswick, and the second unknown craft vanished from radar, possibly landing or descending below radar coverage.
  The next night, on October 28, another unknown aircraft appeared over Loring, this time without lights. Once again, National Guard helicopters were sent to investigate, but were unable to establish visual confirmation of the aircraft. In an Operation Report (OPREP) issued after the incident, officials wrote that “It is our opinion that the unknown helicopter has demonstrated a clear intent in the weapons storage area, is smart and a most capable aviator.” The incidents continued through at least October 30th.
  In response to the incursion, Loring increased its local security presence and coordinated with Canadian authorities to allow U.S. aircraft to pursue the offending aircraft into Canadian airspace should the unknown aircraft return.
 The  New York Times reported on the incursions in the 1979 article "U.F.O. Files: The Untold Story," adding that despite the fact the Joint Chiefs of Staff received multiple briefings on the incursions, "Subsequent investigations by the Air Force into the sightings at Loring Air Force Base, Maine, where the remarkable series of events began, did not reveal a cause for the sightings."
  One account of the aerial intrusions at Loring comes from Arthur Beers, who served at the base from 1970 to 1976. In an account posted to  LoringRemembers.com, a site dedicated to chronicling the experiences of the many men and women who served at Loring, Beers described his “ most memorable experience” at Loring:
 Probably my favorite story was when I was in the Rated Supplement as a Security Police Shift Commander.  I was on duty one evening when the Nuclear Weapons Storage Area was buzzed by a unknown helicopter. Obviously we reported this up channel and I raced out to the storage area. When I arrived, I was told that the National Emergency Command Post was on the line for me. When I talked to the Colonel at the National Command Post and explained what had been reported by my team at the storage area, he gave me orders to shoot down the helicopter if it tried to buzz the area again.
 I immediately went out to the brief my SAT Teams on these orders but before I could complete that briefing I was called back to the entry point where I talked with a General Officer who said that the President had been briefed and that my new orders were to shoot only if the helicopter tried to land (ah, heck!). I went out and rebriefed my teams in just the nick of time as the unknown pilot in an unlighted Huey type helicopter came back again and then two more times during my shift. He came back three other nights and then just disappeared. To my knowledge the identity was never discovered.
 A interesting side note to this, once the information got up to NORAD that we had this unauthorized flying over the storage area, NORAD launched the F-106 that was on alert at Loring. Not exactly sure how that jet jockey was going to see or shoot at a helicopter, 50 feet off the ground in the Northern Maine woods at night running with no lights, but I guess NORAD had to do something. Never saw the F-106, did see the helicopter.
 Other personal testimony complicates the claims that the intruder was actually helicopter. Michael Wallace, a former KC-135 tanker pilot who was stationed at Loring in 1975, shared his own bizarre Loring incursion experience on YouTube:
  Wallace states that he was briefed on an incursion over the nuclear-armed B-52s and weapons storage facilities at Loring. Wallace and a few hundred other personnel were informed of a silent, luminous object hovering over the base which could move "very quickly" and "unconventionally" in "rapid, straight-line movements, with straight vertical movements, can turn without any apparent radius in the turn. It's pretty incredible technology." The object was openly referred to as a UFO by base personnel.
 Wallace goes on to claim that Loring personnel were briefed only to speak to SAC officers about the incursion, not to speak to the press, and that they were "going to tell the press that there was a Canadian helicopter crossing the border and harassing us." He also notes that interceptors were going to be brought in to assist in the efforts to protect the base and investigate what was going on.
 Wallace was eventually sent on a refueling mission in his KC-135 to support F-4 Phantoms for unrelated training when his flight was notified by the base's command post to switch radio frequencies. The lead KC-135 in the flight was instructed to depart the formation, turn off its lights, go radio silent, and proceed to Loring under its own discretion, something Wallace describes as a highly unusual order.
 Wallace remembers hearing "stressed voices" over the radio as the pilots and tower personnel attempted to track the object as it seemed to fly back and forth over the base at incredible speeds. As quickly as the transmissions began, they ended as tower personnel stated simply "We've lost it." When Wallace later saw the pilot of the lead aircraft who was sent to intercept the object, he told Wallace "I can't talk about it, and you wouldn't believe me if I could talk about it."
  LoringRemembers.com contains numerous  other references to the 1975 incursions, with many former Loring personnel calling the incident  their most memorable experience. One former Field Maintenance Squadron member remembers "the activity on  the flight-line was a frenzy" the night of the incursion.
 John E. Morkavich, who served at Loring from 1972 to 1975,  recounted the following to LoringRemembers.com:
 I was a Hospital Corpsman OR Tech 72-75 and one night in the fall of 73 (I will stand corrected if someone else recalls and has better time line [sic]) the base sirens went off and they said thats [sic] the big one thats [sic] gonna scramble the jets and bombers. About 6 of us got on the roof of the medical barracks and waited for whatever. The base went nuts, claxons, sirens, security police vehicles speeding around lots of flashing lights. Lots of flight line roaring of engines. Well about 3 weeeks [sic] later I was in the Officers Club with Dr John P Sheppard and a couple of pilots were sitting with us. We talked sports and politics then Doc Sheppard asked what the hell happened the other night. They replied with the ubiquitous "Do You Have a Need To Know?" Sheppard said hell yes, so I heard this explanation and both pilots were dead serious.
 There was a UFO that came up on radar out of nowhere and was hovering over East Loring near the weapons storage facility. Aircraft was scrambled to intercept, it was seen visually and tracked on radar. Then the Lt Colonel said. "This damn thing was there one second and gone the next, vanished....then radar analysis showed this bogey was so far away and at such a high altitude.....let me tell you this -"... there is NOTHING on THIS PLANET that can do the things this aircraft or damn UFO did...." Then they got up and walked away from the table. Sheppard and I were stunned and we did not talk about it again. This is no BS and I welcome those who were there and remember.
 LoringRemembers.com even contains a section called " the official UFO story" which states simply "In 1975, a 'UFO' buzzed the WSA over a few nights. Internet lore has stamped this as a true UFO conspiracy involving numerous other bases. The DOD released a declassified report on the incident."
  One of the most thorough investigations of the Loring incidents was conducted by Barry Greenwood and Lawrence Fawcett and included in their  1984 book Clear Intent: The Government Coverup of the UFO Experience. An excerpt from that book containing sections on Loring can be  found online here. It goes into great detail as to what the narrative of events was, at least according to their research. We will pick it up on the second night, but we encourage you to read the whole excerpt.
 On October 28, 1975, at 7:45 P.M., Sgt. Clifton W. Blakeslee and Staff Sgt. William J. Long, both assigned to the 42nd Security Police Squadron, were on duty at the munitions storage area. Along with Sgt. Danny Lewis, both Sgts. Blakeslee and Long spotted what appeared to be the running lights of an aircraft approaching Loring Air Force Base from the north at 3,000 feet. The aircraft did not come closer to Loring than about three miles at this time, and it was observed intermittently for the next hour.
 On first spotting the craft, Sgt. Lewis called the Command Post and advised it that the unknown craft had returned to Loring. Lewis reported that he could see a white flashing light and an amber or orange light. Once again, the Commander, 42nd Bomb Wing responded. Rushing to the area of the storage dump, he observed the unknown craft. He reported seeing a flashing white light and an amber-colored light on the object also. The speed and movement in the air suggested that the craft was a helicopter. From 7:45 P.M. to 8:20 P.M., it was under constant observation, both visually by the personnel in the storage area and electronically by the control tower radar, which showed the craft at a position three miles north of the Loring perimeter.
 The unknown craft would appear and disappear from view, and, at one point, appeared over the end of the runway at an altitude of 150 feet. The object subsequently shut off its lights and reappeared over the weapons storage area, maintaining an altitude of 150 feet.
 At this time, Sgt. Steven Eichner, a crew chief on a B-52 bomber, was working out of a launch truck along with Sgt. R. Jones and other members of the crew. Jones spotted a red and orange object over the flight line. It seemed to be on the other side of the flight line from where the weapons storage area was located. To Eichner and Jones, the object looked like a stretched-out football. It hovered in midair as everyone in the crew stared in awe. As they watched, the object put out its lights and disappeared, but it soon reappeared again over the north end of the runway, moving in jerky motions. It stopped and hovered. Eichner and the rest of the crew jumped into the truck and started to drive toward the object.
 Proceeding down Oklahoma Avenue (which borders the runway), they turned left onto the road that led to the weapons storage area. As they made the turn, they spotted the object about 300 feet in front of them. It seemed to be about five feet in the air and hovered without movement or noise. Exhibiting a reddish-orange color, the object was about four car lengths long. Eichner described what he saw next:
 "The object looked like all the colors were blending together, as if you were looking at a desert scene. You see waves of heat rising off the desert floor. This is what I saw. There were these waves in front of the object and all the colors were blending together. The object was solid and we could not hear any noise coming from it."
 They could not see any doors or windows on the object nor any propellers or engines which would keep the object in the air. Suddenly, the base came alive. Sirens began screaming. Eichner could see numerous blue lights on police vehicles coming down the flight line and runway toward the weapons storage area at high speed. Jones turned and said to the crew, "We better get out of here!" They immediately did. The Security Police did not try to stop them. Their interest was in the object over the storage dump, not in the truck which was in a restricted area. The crew drove the truck back to its original location and watched from there. The scene at the weapons storage area was chaotic, with blue lights rotating around, and the vehicles' searchlight beams shining in all directions.
 The men in the crew decided not to report what they had seen, because they had entered a restricted area and could have been arrested for the violation.
 The object shut off its lights and disappeared, not to be seen again that night. The 42nd Police conducted a security sweep of the weapons storage area inside and out, with no results. Radar had once again briefly tracked the object heading for Grand Falls, New Brunswick, finally losing the unknown at Grand Falls itself.
 Priority messages were sent to the National Military Command Center in Washington, the Chief of Staff of the Air Force, the Air Force Forward Operations Division at Fort Ritchie, Maryland, SAC Headquarters, and the 9th Air Force, 45th Division, advising them that an unknown object had penetrated the base and had been in the nuclear storage area.
 The writers interviewed Chief Warrant Officer Bernard Poulin of the Maine Army National Guard's 112th Medical Company (Air Ambulance), who was tasked with tracking down and identifying the unknown aircraft in a UH-1 as the nightly occurrences wore on in late October. Poulin told Fawcett that despite numerous witnesses seeing and hearing the intruder, his helicopter crew could not get visual confirmation on the aircraft, but gives additional details of his experiencing of walking into the SAC base on high alert for a mysterious intruder:
 "Well, we were launched on the first search mission after ground personnel started to see or hear the, quote, if you will, "UFO" go by. So, we would launch, and I believe that we were in the air for around 40 minutes looking for this thing, with the idea that it was a rotary-type craft we were searching for. We were vectored in by ground personnel to different spots on the base where the ground personnel were seeing or hearing it. All this time we were being tracked by base radar [traffic control radar which is designed to pick up aircraft], and radar was not painting the object that was being reported. Ground personnel would call and say the object is at this location, but radar would not pick it up.
 Well, anyway, we hunted around, and we didn't see anything. Again they would call and say they could hear it at a location, and we would go there, but could not see it. We would then shut down and wait for the next call. And that went on for a couple of nights. This, again, was early evening or early in the morning. I can recall on the second night of the mission radar picked up a return, but it turned out to be a KC-135 tanker returning from overseas."  Poulin was asked: "According to some of the documents, personnel on the ground were reporting that at times you would bring your craft within 100 feet of the intruder, yet you could not see it?" He answered:  "Yes, well, we could go real low to where they said it was and would turn on our search light and sweep the area with the light, but we never saw the craft. After it was over, we discussed our mission. The powers to be were quite concerned about what was going on and if we were able to see anything. They maintained all along up there, you know, those are pretty sensitive places and they have to know what the hell was going on."
 When they arrived at the base, the security lid was on so tightly that both pilots were permitted to call their wives only once to say that they were on a mission. In a meeting with Chapman, Poulin recalled the Commander saying, "We've got to keep the lid on the fact that someone has been able to penetrate in and around the bomb dump, and we don't know what's going on. We've got to find out what is going on and prevent it from happening again."
  At Loring, additional manpower was armed and ready for deployment. The Security Police Battle Staff was to be manned at Central Security Control. An additional two-man mobile patrol was assigned to the weapons storage area during the hours of darkness, while a ten-man reserve force was standing by, ready for deployment. A two-man patrol would be positioned at key vantage points about one mile north of the base for added surveillance. An SAC/SP message informed northern tier bases of the situation and recommended a "Security Option Three" alert all along the U.S.-Canadian border.
 The message went to Pease AFB in New Hampshire, Plattsburgh AFB in New York, Wurtsmith AFB in Michigan, Kinchloe AFB in Michigan, Sawyer AFB in Michigan, Grand Forks AFB in North Dakota, Minot AFB in North Dakota, Malmstrom AFB in Montana, Fairchild AFB in Washington, and Barksdale AFB in Iowa. The subject-identifying line of the message was "Defense Against Helicopter Assault," and it read:
 The past two evenings at one of our northern tier bases, an unidentified helicopter has been observed hovering over and in the near vicinity of the weapons storage area. Attempts to identify this aircraft have so far met with negative results. In the interest of nuclear weapons security, the acti

title: 不明身份飞机坠落洛林空军基地的神秘冷战案件
title_en: The Mysterious Cold War Case of Unidentified Aircraft Descending on Loring AFB

note:
注册我们的特别版时事通讯，获得关于冠状病毒大流行的每日最新消息。
根据本周公布的调查结果，一名内华达州居民被认为是美国第一个两次感染冠状病毒的人。
根据周四发布在SSRN网站上的一项研究，这名25岁的男子来自里诺，经历了喉咙痛、咳嗽、头痛、恶心和腹泻-4月18日首次新冠肺炎检测呈阳性，该研究尚未得到同行审查。
研究称，他的症状在4月27日之前已经缓解，他在5月份有两次病毒检测呈阴性。
根据这项研究，5月31日，除了发烧和头晕外，他再次因同样的症状寻求治疗。
患者在五天后住院，因为他的症状恶化，包括肌肉疼痛，咳嗽和呼吸急促。
然后，他第二次检测出冠状病毒呈阳性。另一项测试显示他有抗感染的抗体。
内华达州州立公共卫生实验室(Nevada State Public Health Laboratory)主任马克·潘多里(Mark Pandori)博士在一份声明中表示，一个人“在如此短的时间内”再次感染的事实表明，“为对抗这种疾病而开发的疫苗的效力可能会受到影响”，也可能会影响群体免疫力。
然而，他说，“重要的是要注意到，这是一个奇特的发现。”
“它没有向我们提供任何关于这种现象的普遍性的信息，”潘多里补充说。
在这项新的研究之前，研究人员周一透露，一名33岁的香港男子是有史以来第一个被证实再次感染冠状病毒的患者。
本周还有消息称，另外两名冠状病毒患者，一名来自荷兰，一名来自比利时，再次感染了这种传染病。
note_en:
Sign up for our  special edition newsletter to get a daily update on the coronavirus pandemic.
    A Nevada resident is thought to be the first person in the United States to be infected twice by the coronavirus, according to findings released this week.
 The 25-year-old man, of Reno, experienced a sore throat, cough, headache, nausea and diarrhea — and first tested positive for the COVID-19 on April 18, according to a study  published to the website SSRN Thursday, which has yet to be peer-reviewed.
 His symptoms had resolved by April 27, and he tested negative for the virus twice in May, the study says.
 Then on May 31, he sought treatment again for the same symptoms, in addition to a fever and dizziness, according to the study.
 The patient was hospitalized five days later as his symptoms worsened to include muscle aches, a cough and shortness of breath.
 He then tested positive for the coronavirus a second time. Another test revealed he had antibodies against the infection.
 Dr. Mark Pandori, director of the Nevada State Public Health Laboratory,  said in a statement that the fact that a person was reinfected “on such a short timeline” indicates “there may be implications for the efficacy of vaccines developed to fight the disease,” as well as for herd immunity.
 However, he said it’s “important to note that this is a singular finding.”
 “It does not provide any information to us with regard to the generalizability of this phenomenon,” Pandori added.
    The new study comes after researchers on Monday revealed a 33-year-old Hong Kong man  was the first patient ever confirmed to be reinfected with the coronavirus.
 It also emerged this week that two other coronavirus patients, one from the Netherlands and one from Belgium,  were reinfected with the contagion.
title: 内华达州男子成为美国第一个两次抓获新冠肺炎的人
title_en: Nevada Man Becomes First In the US To Catch COVID-19 Twice

8月初，白俄罗斯--有时被称为欧洲最后的独裁政权--几乎完全断电72小时。8月26日星期三，白俄罗斯再次关闭了首都互联网的关键部分，持续了大约一个小时；据称，这一命令直接来自官方国家机构。
早些时候的停电中断了整个抗议国家的通信，但主要通过次要电报频道NEXTA Live播放的缓慢片段播放了防暴警察袭击和平抗议者，并部署橡皮子弹和眩晕手榴弹；从阳台拍摄的电话视频显示，警卫在监狱院子里暴力殴打在押人员。随着这个国家逐渐恢复网络，示威者深红蓝色瘀伤的描述，以及施虐酷刑、强奸威胁和性侵犯的指控开始在社交网络和新闻网站上流传。在冲突的中心，加密的应用程序Telegram在广泛的代理和虚拟专用网络使用的帮助下，成为传播信息和抗议组织的关键。
随着白俄罗斯的互联网中断，我们看到了当过度依赖集中化的互联网和少数几家大公司遇到国家审查时会发生什么，以及可以采取什么措施-如果有的话-可以做些什么。
“当他们关闭互联网时，…。我们不知道发生了什么，“一位名叫阿莱娜(Alena)的抗议者通过Facebook Messenger说。“当有关暴力的信息开始[传播]时，我愤慨得浑身发抖--殴打、攻击、侮辱、枪击…。所有这些现在都充斥着互联网……。我们都感到集体震惊。“。
另一名抗议者基里拉(化名)在谈到Reddit Messenger上史无前例的警察暴行图片和讨论时说：“当互联网重新出现时，它是有趣的-同时也是恐怖和自豪的。”(为了保护自己的身份，这里的抗议者只透露了名字和化名。)。
白俄罗斯的抗议浪潮最初是在该国65岁的领导人亚历山大·卢卡申科(Alexander Lukashenko)宣布在8月9日的选举中赢得80%的胜利后开始的，卢卡申科自1994年以来一直担任国家主席，这一说法受到抗议者和外国观察人士的广泛质疑。反对派候选人斯维特拉娜·蒂哈努斯卡娅(Svetlana Tikhanouskaya)宣布获胜，但后来离开该国前往邻国立陶宛，显然是在胁迫下。多家媒体报道，数千名抗议者在冲突中被拘留，至少一人死亡，数十人受伤；这一报道得到了这个前苏联国家大量公民文件的支持。在72小时的时间里，人们试图对倾泻的水进行审查。
“从8月9日…开始。上午9点左右，互联网开始关闭，“白俄罗斯欧洲人文大学传播和发展部门负责人Maksimas Milta回忆道，该大学目前流亡在立陶宛。当第一批网站遭到攻击时，米尔塔正在明斯克：一个追踪投票的独立平台Golos，以及一个允许用户报告选举舞弊事件的众包平台Zubr。
“…的殴打，攻击，侮辱，枪击”所有这些现在都充斥着互联网……。我们都感到集体震惊。“
米尔塔说：“两个小时后，YouTube的访问基本上被封锁了，为了让人们无法观看流媒体，然后整个互联网开始关闭。”谷歌、Facebook和WhatsApp都遭遇了访问问题，独立新闻来源也是如此。白俄罗斯电信提供商为大规模停电表示道歉，最终，端到端加密通讯应用Telegram成为仅存的服务之一。
“我们在白俄罗斯启用了我们的反审查工具，以便Telegram在那里的大多数用户仍然可以使用。然而，连接仍然非常不稳定，因为这个国家的互联网有时会被完全切断，“Telegram创始人帕维尔·杜罗夫(Pavel Durov)8月10日在Twitter上写道。
NEXTA Live Telegram频道由22岁的Steban Putilo运营，总部设在波兰，其用户群在几天内就爆炸式增长。在抗议活动开始时，只有数十万人知道它的存在。现在，它有200多万订户。
他告诉德国媒体Tagesschau，NEXTA对当局构成了足够明显的威胁，根据其年轻的创始人的说法，Putilo在白俄罗斯和俄罗斯都是“通缉”的，并面临最高15年的监禁。普蒂罗不会公开透露他在华沙的位置，理由是威胁和仇恨信息。普蒂罗在接受采访时说：“总统选举期间，我的家人、母亲和兄弟还在白俄罗斯，但之后他们从官员那里得到暗示，在白俄罗斯可能会让他们感到不舒服。”“他们现在也在波兰。”
随着抗议组织继续依赖这些以前的利基平台，该国的VPN使用率也出现了激增。8月9日左右，白俄罗斯的Google Play Store将Psiphon、X-VPN、Tachyon VPN和VPN Proxy Master列为下载次数最多的四款应用程序，该国其他人表示，他们付费购买了Surfshark或使用了Shadowsock，后者最出名的是绕过了中国的长城防火墙。
“你会看到电梯里的人只是把U盘和VPN访问文件放在一起。”
Psiphon的迈克尔·赫尔(Michael Hull)表示：“8月11日，Psiphon的白俄罗斯独立用户达到了175.9万人的峰值。从8月9日开始，我们看到了广泛、持续的使用情况。”Psiphon的使用增加是因为口碑，而不是营销…。口碑是极其强大的，“他补充说。
基层的单独行为者正在为这些VPN的使用提供便利。“你会看到电梯里的人只是把U盘和VPN访问文件放在一起。这很有趣，这就像是低技术在起作用，“米尔塔说。“一些人不知道VPN，所以有几个人离线了三天，”Kirila补充道。“我甚至有过在VPN上分发带有说明的列表的想法，但就在我想出这个想法的同一天晚上，互联网又回来了。”
“在官方国家机构的命令下，从8月26日20时40分起，明斯克的移动互联网带宽将受到限制。(我们)遵守这一要求将导致数据传输质量下降或出现暂时性服务故障，“移动网络提供商A1周三在推特上写道。
虽然8月份出现了不同类型的网络挑战，但政府试图利用早先的问题。卢卡申科最初将互联网中断归咎于“外国网络攻击”，而白俄罗斯国家计算机事件响应中心则指控DDoS攻击政府基础设施。然而，白俄罗斯的互联网通过一种名为深度包检测的方法崩溃。DPI攻击基于实际的域名，因此Telegram绕过了它们，受益于使用IP地址而不是域名。
DPI在网络安全圈子之外更为人所知的是“包嗅探”，用于监视包的去向，这对监控敏感网络上的流量很有用，但也经常用于审查。据追踪在线中断和关闭的NetBlock称，伊朗也使用了DPI。政府似乎使用了列出的关键字，可以用来阻止对特定URL的访问。“DPI是用来过滤域名的--它可以过滤协议，但这是可以解决的，”NetBlock首席执行官阿尔普·托克(ALP Toker)说。
他补充说：“在限制方面，有两种主要机制在发挥作用-链路层/网络层中断-互联网路由的大块在不同时间中断，以及DPI。”Telegram之所以能够工作，是因为它根本不使用域名--它直接使用IP地址。托克说：“他们非常擅长从一个地址跳转到另一个地址，所以如果一个网络出现故障，他们会自动切换，或者只需让用户输入新的设置来连接到另一台服务器。”
以科技为主的白俄罗斯经济因停电而受到严重打击，据报道，每天损失高达5600万美元。2000多名投资者、高管和科技行业工人签署了一封公开信，称该国的条件意味着他们的企业无法运作。“初创公司不是在恐惧和暴力的氛围中诞生的。初创公司诞生于自由和开放的氛围中，“他们表示，预计增长将放缓，甚至会出现大规模外流。
8月23日，挥之不去的互联网问题仍然存在，白俄罗斯的用户显然永远无法访问几个独立的新闻网站。那天，也就是互联网关闭两周后，数十万人无畏地走上街头，再次高呼“辞职！”在卢卡申科，挂着前苏联白红相间的白俄罗斯国旗的颜色。当晚，当抗议者接近总统府时，明斯克各地的蜂窝网络MTS都下线了，NetBlock指出：“运营商A1随后宣布，应政府机构的要求，服务质量已经降低，以‘确保国家安全’。”这些问题持续了大约三个小时。
周日，NEXTA电报频道再次发挥了关键作用。“警方在明斯克的所有入口处都设置了检查站。白俄罗斯看起来越来越像一个战区，“NEXTA写道。该频道负责向参与者传播方向，从“带水”和“明天可能会下雨，所以不要忘了雨衣或雨伞”等基本实用知识，到帮助人们在首都明斯克郊外组织起来，并就如何处理被交警拦截的问题提出建议。
上周末的抗议者挥舞着描绘前几周警察暴行的图片。“许多人带来了冲突中受伤人员的照片，”弗兰纳克通过驻明斯克的大西洋理事会研究员čOrka写道。这些照片最初在互联网中断期间被审查，现在到处都是。
而推特谴责白俄罗斯的互联网中断，称“互联网关闭是非常有害的。他们从根本上侵犯了基本人权&而且违反了#开放互联网的原则“，当联系到他们时，他们不会提供更多的信息。WhatsApp/Facebook表示，它不希望就该公司干预潜在威权压迫局势的更广泛责任公开发表言论。谷歌没有回应记者的置评请求。
电子前沿基金会(Electronic Frontier Foundation)国际言论自由主任吉莉安·C·约克(Jillian C.York)表示，规模较小的公司正在越来越多地填补科技巨头留下的空白--不仅是在白俄罗斯，在国际上也是如此。约克说：“我认为，我们将看到更多的人转向更小的平台和更安全的组织平台，但在传播信息方面就不那么重要了。”他表示，人们在Facebook上保留了与现有网络的更强链接。在全球范围内，更大的社交网络，如Facebook，似乎偏向于“一般的政府而不是人民”。
“我认为，我们将看到更多向更小平台和更安全的组织平台发展的趋势……”
较小的公司可以在人们经历某种程度的极权控制的国家提供各种服务，并可以更好地促进受压迫公民的匿名协调。在规避国家强加的限制或技术隐私问题的同时，通过私人渠道进行广播可能是抵制的关键。
反过来，俄罗斯过去也曾试图禁止电报本身。它在6月份解除了近两年(极其无效)的禁令。Telegram表示，它拒绝与该国通信监管机构Roskomnadzor分享其加密密钥。2月份，Twitter和Facebook也因拒绝将用户数据存储在俄罗斯境内的服务器上而在俄罗斯遭遇400万卢布(约合6.3万美元)的罚款。
当然，像Telegram这样的平台也有自己的问题，即使它们接待了协调抗议活动的白俄罗斯青年。端到端加密是为“秘密”聊天保留的，不适用于群发消息。它的服务器代码是封闭源代码的(只有客户端是开源的)，这引起了一些安全方面的怀疑；其他消息传递应用程序也将其服务器代码开源。与任何其他社交网络一样，它也容易受到用户的错误信息和虚假信息的影响，以及潜在的严重安全漏洞。Telegram只是一种选择，很难说是一个次要的参与者。但用更小、治理更少的技术取代美国传统上使用的应用程序和服务，将允许独立的渠道更有效地组织和分发信息。在面对任何潜在的镇压或互联网审查时，这可能是朝着保护真相和透明度的正确方向迈出的一步。
在撰写本文时，明斯克的抗议活动正在被驱散，一些抗议者被封锁在一座教堂里。据报道，卢卡申科正试图“逐步”平息抗议活动。NEXTA继续更新和组织其200万成员。白俄罗斯的互联网连接充其量也是参差不齐。
艾丽德·内勒是“东方的阴影：弗拉基米尔·普京和新波罗的海阵线”(I.B.Tauris，2020)一书的作者。
请不要给川普任何想法。我们最不需要的就是11月份的“滚动停电”。
note_en:
In early August, Belarus—sometimes called Europe’s last dictatorship—went almost entirely offline for 72 hours. On Wednesday, Aug. 26,   for approximately one hour, Belarus   shut down key parts of the capital’s internet once again; allegedly, the order had come directly from official state bodies.
 The earlier outage disrupted communication across the protesting nation, but a slow trickle of footage, largely via minor Telegram channel NEXTA Live,   broadcasted riot police   attacking peaceful protesters and deploying rubber bullets and stun grenades; phone videos shot from balconies revealed guards   violently beating detainees in prison yards. As the country gradually came back online, depictions of protesters’ dark red-blue   bruising, as well as claims of   sadistic torture,   rape threats, and   sexual assault began circulating on social networks and news sites. In the center of the conflict, the encrypted app Telegram, aided by a wide range of proxies and virtual private network usage, became pivotal in both disseminating information and protest organization.
 With the internet outages in Belarus, we see just what can happen when an over-dependence on centralized internet and a select few major companies meets national censorship, and what—if anything—can be done about it.
  “When they turned off the internet… we didn’t know what was happening,” said one protester, Alena, over Facebook messenger. “I shook with indignation when information about the violence began [to spread]—the beatings, assaults, insults, shootings… All of this has now flooded the internet... We are all in collective shock.”
 “When the internet was back, it was interesting—horror and pride at the same time,” another protester, Kirila (a pseudonym), said of the unprecedented images and discussions of police brutality via Reddit messenger. (The protesters are identified here by first name and pseudonym only, to protect their identities.)
 The wave of protests in Belarus first began after its 65-year-old leader, Alexander Lukashenko, who has presided over the nation since 1994, claimed to have won an 80% victory in elections on Aug. 9. This was widely contested by protesters and foreign observers. Opposition candidate Svetlana Tikhanouskaya claimed the victory, but later left the country for neighboring Lithuania, apparently under duress. Multiple outlets reported thousands of protesters detained, at least one person killed, and dozens injured in the clashes; the reporting was supported by a mass of citizen documentation from the former Soviet nation. For 72 hours, attempts were made to censor the outpour.
   “Starting on August 9… somewhere around 9:00 am, the internet started to be shut down,” recalled Maksimas Milta, Head of Communication and Development Unit at the Belarusian European Humanities University, the university now exiled and based in Lithuania. Milta was on the ground in Minsk when the first websites were attacked: an independent platform for tracking voting, Golos, and a crowdsourcing platform which allows users to report incidences of electoral fraud, Zubr.
 “The beatings, assaults, insults, shootings… All of this has now flooded the internet... We are all in collective shock.”
 “Two hours later, basically YouTube access was blocked, in order for people not to be able to watch streams, then the entire internet started to be shut down,” said Milta. Google, Facebook, and WhatsApp all suffered access issues, as did independent news sources. Belarusian telecom providers issued   apologies for mass outages, and ultimately, the end-to-end encrypted messaging app Telegram was one of the only services left.
 “We enabled our anti-censorship tools in Belarus so that Telegram remained available for most users there. However, the connection is still very unstable as internet is at times shut off completely in the country,” Telegram founder Pavel Durov   wrote on Twitter on Aug. 10.
 The NEXTA Live Telegram channel, run by 22-year-old Stepan Putilo and based in Poland, saw its user base explode in a matter of days. At the beginning of the protests, only a few hundred thousand people knew of its existence. Now, it has more than 2 million subscribers.
  NEXTA poses enough of an apparent threat to the authorities that according to its young founder, Putilo is “wanted” in both Belarus and Russia and facing up to 15 years imprisonment, he told the German outlet   Tagesschau. Putilo will not publicly reveal his location in Warsaw, citing threats and hate messages. “My family, my mother and my brother were still in Belarus during the presidential elections, but then they received a hint from officials that it could be uncomfortable for them in Belarus,” Putilo said in the interview. “They are now in Poland too.”
 As protest organizations continue to depend on these previously niche platforms, the country is also seeing a spike in VPN usage. Around Aug. 9, the Google Play Store in Belarus listed Psiphon, X-VPN, Tachyon VPN, and VPN Proxy Master in its top four app downloads, with others in the country saying they paid for Surfshark or used Shadowsocks, best known for circumventing the great firewall in China.
 “You would see people in the elevator just leaving USB sticks with VPN access files.”
 “Psiphon saw a peak of 1.759 million unique users from Belarus on August 11. We have seen extensive, sustained use starting August 9,” said Psiphon’s Michael Hull. “Psiphon use increased due to word of mouth, not marketing … word of mouth is extremely powerful,” he added.
 Access to these VPNs is being facilitated by lone actors at a grassroots level. “You would see people in the elevator just leaving USB sticks with VPN access files. It’s funny, this is like low tech in action,” said Milta. “Some didn’t know about VPN, so there were a few people offline for three days,” added Kirila. “I even had the idea to spread lists with instructions on VPNs, but on the same night I came up with that idea, the internet was back.”
    “At the orders of official state bodies, from 20:40 on August 26 in Minsk mobile internet bandwidth will be restricted. [Our] compliance with this requirement will lead to a decline in the quality of data transmission or temporary service failures,” mobile network provider A1   tweeted Wednesday.
 While there were different types of network challenges across August, the government attempted to play off earlier issues. Lukashenko, for his part, originally blamed   “foreign cyberattacks” for the internet outages, while the National Computer Incident Response Center of Belarus   alleged DDoS attacks on government infrastructure. However, Belarus’ internet went down via a method called Deep Packet Inspection. DPI attacks were based around actual domain names, so Telegram bypassed them, benefitting from using IP addresses instead of domains.
 DPI is more commonly known as “packet sniffing” outside cyber security circles and is used for watching where packets go, which is useful for monitoring traffic on sensitive networks, but is often also used for censorship. DPI was also used in Iran, according to NetBlocks, which tracks online disruptions and shutdowns. It appeared that the government employed listed keywords that it could use to block access to specific URLs. “DPI is used for filtering domain name[s]—it can filter protocols but that can be worked around,” said Alp Toker, NetBlocks CEO.
  “Two primary mechanisms at play here when it comes to the restrictions—the link layer/network layer disruptions—the chunk of the internet route being disrupted at different times, and the DPI,” he added. Telegram was able to work because it doesn’t use domain names at all—it uses IP addresses directly. “They’re quite good at leaping from address to address, so if one network goes down they switch over either automatically or just by having users enter a new setting to connect to another server,” said Toker.
 The tech-heavy Belarusian economy took a severe hit as a result of the outages, reportedly losing as much as $56 million each day. More than 2,000 investors, executives, and tech sector workers signed an open letter saying conditions in the country meant their businesses could not function. “Startups are not born in an atmosphere of fear and violence. Startups are born in an atmosphere of freedom and openness,” they   stated, anticipating a slowdown in growth and even a mass exodus.
  Lingering internet problems remained on Aug. 23, with several independent news websites apparently permanently unavailable for users in Belarus. On that day, two weeks after the internet shutdown, hundreds of thousands fearlessly took to the streets once again chanting “Resign!” at Lukashenko, bearing the colors of the pre-Soviet white-and-red Belarusian flag. In the evening, when cellular network MTS fell offline across Minsk as protesters approached the presidential palace, NetBlocks   noted: “Operator A1 subsequently declared service quality had been reduced at the request of government agencies to ‘ensure national security.’” The issues lasted for around three hours.
 The NEXTA Telegram channel once again played a pivotal role on Sunday. “Police set up checkpoints at all entrances to Minsk. Belarus looks more and more like a war zone,” NEXTA wrote. The channel was responsible for spreading   directions to participants, from basic practicalities such as “take water” and “tomorrow it might be rainy, so don’t forget raincoats or umbrellas” to helping people organize outside the capital, Minsk, and suggesting tips for how to deal with being stopped by traffic police.
 The past weekend’s protesters wielded images depicting the previous weeks’ police brutality. “Many people brought photos of injured people during the clashes,”   wrote Franak Viačorka, an Atlantic Council fellow based in Minsk. These photos, originally censored during the internet blackout, were now everywhere.
  While Twitter   condemned the internet outages in Belarus, stating that “Internet shutdowns are hugely harmful. They fundamentally violate basic human rights & the principles of the   #OpenInternet,” it would not provide further information when contacted. WhatsApp/Facebook said that it did not wish to speak on the record regarding the company’s broader responsibility to intervene in situations of potential authoritarian oppression. Google was unresponsive to requests for comment.
 Smaller companies are increasingly filling the gaps left by the tech behemoths—not just in Belarus but on an international level, said Jillian C. York, director of international freedom of expression at the Electronic Frontier Foundation. “I think we’ll see a lot more movement towards smaller platforms and safer platforms for organizing but less so for getting the word out,” York said, suggesting people retained stronger links to their existing networks on Facebook. On a global scale the bigger social networks such as Facebook appear to have a bias towards “governments in general over the people.”
 “I think we’ll see a lot more movement towards smaller platforms and safer platforms for organizing...”
  Smaller companies can provide a variety of services in nations where people experience some level of totalitarian control and can better facilitate anonymous coordination of oppressed citizens. Broadcasts via private channels while circumventing state-imposed restrictions or technological privacy issues can be pivotal to resistance.
 In turn, Russia has attempted to prohibit Telegram itself in the past. It lifted its nearly two-year (and wildly ineffectual) ban in June. Telegram has said that it refused to share its encryption keys with Roskomnadzor, the country’s communications watchdog. Twitter and Facebook also suffered fines of 4 million rubles (around $63,000) in Russia in February for refusing to store user data on servers inside the country.
 Of course, platforms like Telegram come with their own issues, even as they host Belarusian youth coordinating protest efforts. End-to-end encryption is reserved for “secret” chats and doesn’t apply to group messages. Its server code is closed-source (only its client side is open-source) which has raised some security suspicions; other messaging apps make their server code open-source too. It is also susceptible to misinformation and disinformation from its users like any other social network, as well as potential   serious   security   flaws. Telegram is just one alternative, and hardly a minor player. But substituting the apps and services traditionally used in the U.S. with smaller, less-governed technology would allow independent channels to organize and distribute information more effectively. This may be a step in the right direction in protecting truth and transparency in the face of any potential crackdowns or internet censorship.
 As of this writing, the protests in Minsk are being dispersed, with some protesters   blockaded in a church. Lukashenko, reportedly, is attempting to subdue the protests   “gradually.” NEXTA continues to update and organize its 2 million members. Internet connectivity in Belarus remains spotty at best.
 Aliide Naylor is the author of The Shadow in the East: Vladimir Putin and the New Baltic Front (I.B. Tauris, 2020).
       Please don’t give trump any ideas. The last thing we need are “rolling outages” in November.
title: 当白俄罗斯的互联网使用深度包检测关闭时，白俄罗斯人是如何转向VPN和专用电报通道来绕过审查的
title_en: How Belarusians turned to VPNs and a private Telegram channel to bypass censorship when its internet was shut down using deep packet inspection
